summary: A simple in-memory user management service exposing REST endpoints to create users and fully replace existing user records, with system-generated UUID identifiers and unique email addresses.

groups.name: User Management
groups.summary: Capabilities for creating and fully replacing User resources with a fixed id/name/email schema, UUID generation, and email uniqueness enforced in an in-memory store.

requirements.title: Create User endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST endpoint that creates a User from a supplied name and email.

acceptance.given: a creation request containing valid values for name and email
acceptance.outcome: a new User is persisted with a system-generated UUID as its id, and a success response is returned

acceptance.given: a creation request that does not include a name
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a creation request that does not include an email
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a creation request that includes an email already held by a persisted User
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request to preserve email uniqueness

acceptance.given: a creation request that includes extra fields beyond name and email
acceptance.outcome: the persisted User contains only id, name, and email; any additional fields are discarded

requirements.title: Replace User endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST endpoint that fully replaces an existing persisted User with a complete User record.

acceptance.given: an existing User and a replace request containing a complete User record including id, name, and email
acceptance.outcome: the stored User is fully replaced with the supplied record, and a success response is returned

acceptance.given: a replace request referencing an id that does not identify any persisted User
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request because the target User does not exist

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing User where the request body is missing name, email, or id
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a replace request where the id in the request body does not match the targeted User's id
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing User that repeats the User's own current email
acceptance.outcome: the replacement succeeds and the User remains uniquely represented

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing User that specifies an email already held by a different User
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request to preserve email uniqueness

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing User that includes extra fields beyond id, name, and email
acceptance.outcome: the persisted User contains only id, name, and email; any additional fields are discarded

requirements.title: UUID identifier generation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL generate a UUID for the User id upon creation and SHALL NOT allow clients to supply or modify it.

acceptance.given: a User creation request
acceptance.outcome: the id value is a valid, system-generated UUID

acceptance.given: two independent User creation requests
acceptance.outcome: each User is assigned a distinct id

acceptance.given: a creation request that includes a client-supplied id
acceptance.outcome: the system ignores the supplied id and assigns its own UUID

acceptance.given: an existing User
acceptance.outcome: the User's id remains unchanged from the value assigned at creation

requirements.title: Email uniqueness
requirements.statement: The system SHALL enforce that the email field is unique across all persisted Users for both creation and replacement operations.

acceptance.given: a User already exists with a specific email address
acceptance.when: a creation request specifies that same email
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a User already exists with a specific email address
acceptance.when: a replace request for a different User specifies that same email
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a User already exists with a specific email address
acceptance.when: a replace request for that same User repeats the current email
acceptance.outcome: the replacement succeeds and uniqueness is maintained

acceptance.given: no persisted User has a specific email address
acceptance.when: a creation or replace request specifies that email
acceptance.outcome: the operation succeeds and the new email is stored

requirements.title: User representation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL represent a User with exactly the fields id, name, and email, and SHALL NOT persist additional fields supplied by clients.

acceptance.given: any persisted User
acceptance.outcome: it contains exactly the fields id, name, and email

acceptance.given: a User has been created or replaced
acceptance.outcome: no extra computed or metadata fields are present beyond id, name, and email

requirements.title: In-memory persistence
requirements.statement: The system SHALL use an in-memory store for all User persistence.

acceptance.given: a User has been persisted
acceptance.when: the running process serves subsequent requests
acceptance.outcome: the User record remains available and can be replaced within the same process instance

acceptance.given: a User has been persisted
acceptance.outcome: the previously persisted User records are no longer present

groups.name: Bear CRUD
groups.summary: Capabilities for creating, reading, listing, updating, and deleting Bear resources with id/name/age/colour schema, UUIDv7 generation, validation rules for name and age, freeform colour, and cursor-based pagination backed by in-memory storage.

requirements.title: Create Bear
requirements.statement: The system SHALL create a bear record from a client-provided name, age, and colour, assigning a service-generated UUIDv7 identifier and returning the complete record.

acceptance.given: a non-empty, unique name no longer than 100 characters that also satisfies applicable character/pattern restrictions, a non-negative integer age, and a freeform colour
acceptance.outcome: the system stores the bear and returns the record including the generated UUIDv7 identifier

acceptance.given: a create request with an empty name
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a create request with a name that exceeds 100 characters
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a create request with a name that duplicates an existing bear name
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a create request with a name that violates applicable character/pattern restrictions
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a create request with a negative age
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a create request with an age that is not an integer
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

requirements.title: Read Bear
requirements.statement: The system SHALL retrieve a single bear by its UUIDv7 identifier.

acceptance.given: a bear exists with a known UUIDv7 identifier
acceptance.when: a read request is made for that identifier
acceptance.outcome: the system returns the corresponding bear record

acceptance.given: a read request for a UUIDv7 identifier that does not exist
acceptance.outcome: the system indicates that the bear was not found

requirements.title: List Bears
requirements.statement: The system SHALL list all stored bears using cursor-based pagination.

acceptance.given: one or more bears exist in storage
acceptance.outcome: the system returns a paginated result set using cursor-based pagination

acceptance.given: no bears exist
acceptance.outcome: the system returns an empty paginated result set

requirements.title: Update Bear
requirements.statement: The system SHALL update an existing bear, identified by UUIDv7, by fully replacing its mutable fields (name, age, colour).

acceptance.given: an existing bear and a replacement representation that satisfies all creation validation rules
acceptance.outcome: the system overwrites the bear's mutable fields and returns the updated record

acceptance.given: an update request with an invalid age
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the update

acceptance.given: an update request with a duplicate name
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the update

acceptance.given: an update request with an empty name
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the update

acceptance.given: an update request that omits one or more mutable fields
acceptance.outcome: the system applies full replacement semantics and stores exactly the provided values

requirements.title: Delete Bear
requirements.statement: The system SHALL delete a bear by its UUIDv7 identifier.

acceptance.given: an existing bear
acceptance.when: a delete request is made by identifier
acceptance.outcome: the system removes the bear from storage

acceptance.given: a delete request for a UUIDv7 identifier that does not exist
acceptance.outcome: the system indicates that the bear was not found

requirements.title: Bear in-memory persistence
requirements.statement: The system SHALL retain all bear records in volatile, in-memory storage with no persistence across process restarts.

acceptance.given: bears have been created in the service
acceptance.outcome: no bear records remain

requirements.title: In-memory operation latency
requirements.statement: The system SHALL process all CRUD operations with latency consistent with in-memory data access and without reliance on external persistence services.

acceptance.given: bear CRUD operations are executed
acceptance.outcome: responses are produced without reliance on external persistence services and with latency consistent with in-memory access

requirements.title: Local UUIDv7 generation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL generate UUIDv7 identifiers using only local runtime capabilities.

acceptance.given: the service generates a bear identifier
acceptance.outcome: the identifier is produced using only local runtime capabilities without depending on external network services

rules.rule: A User id is immutable and is system-assigned as a UUID at creation time.
rules.rationale: Guarantees unique identity without requiring client coordination and prevents identity spoofing or alteration.

rules.rule: The email address acts as a unique key across the entire user population.
rules.rationale: Prevents duplicate accounts and ambiguous identity lookups.

rules.rule: Replacement of a User is a full-record operation requiring id, name, and email; partial updates are not permitted.
rules.rationale: Avoids partial-update ambiguity and keeps the state transfer contract explicit.

rules.rule: A User entity has exactly the fields id, name, and email.
rules.rationale: Keeps the domain model minimal and predictable for clients.

rules.rule: User data persists only in memory for the lifetime of the process; no durable storage or cross-process replication is provided.
rules.rationale: Matches the explicit in-memory store requirement and sets clear durability expectations.

rules.rule: A Bear id is immutable and is system-assigned as a UUIDv7 value at creation time; clients cannot supply or modify it.
rules.rationale: Guarantees unique, ordered identity without client coordination and prevents identity spoofing or alteration.

rules.rule: A Bear name acts as a unique key across the entire bear collection.
rules.rationale: Prevents duplicate bear records and ambiguous identification.

rules.rule: A Bear age is strictly a non-negative integer; fractional or negative values are prohibited.
rules.rationale: Ensures age is stored as a valid whole number that represents a meaningful duration.

rules.rule: A Bear name must be non-empty, must not exceed 100 characters, and is subject to additional applicable character/pattern restrictions.
rules.rationale: Maintains data quality and consistent naming constraints.

rules.rule: A Bear colour is freeform text with no predefined enumeration, format, or length constraint.
rules.rationale: Allows clients to capture colour values flexibly without constraining the domain model.

rules.rule: Bear updates use full replacement semantics, meaning the request representation entirely supersedes the previous mutable state of the resource.
rules.rationale: Avoids partial-update ambiguity and keeps the state transfer contract explicit.

rules.rule: Bear data persists only in memory for the lifetime of the process; no durable storage or cross-process replication is provided.
rules.rationale: Matches the explicit in-memory store requirement and sets clear durability expectations.
